opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was changing my shirt and noticed this dead, white, shiny skin on my shoulder. It looks completely abnormal, like my flesh is dying, and I'm terrified it's spreading.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I found it tonight and panicked that it's an infection eating my skin or spreading fast.

symptom_narrative.character: a shiny, thin, porcelain-white patch; painless
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: no pain or itch, but very frightening
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: since I noticed it and started rubbing it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: rubbing it (now a red ring around it)
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no pain, no itch, no fever; a red ring appeared after I rubbed it

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: has been rubbing/inspecting the area repeatedly

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to be told her skin isn't dying/spreading

objective_data_offered.value: a shiny, thin, porcelain-white patch on the back of the shoulder with a faint red ring around it (from rubbing); the surface looks slightly wrinkled like cigarette paper; no black areas, no open sore, no pus
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: phone-camera view; cannot palpate induration/depth or biopsy remotely
